item.title: Sen. Rand Paul's son confronted Rep. Mike Lawler in drunken, antisemitic rant
item.description: The senator's son apologized Wednesday, saying he was seeking help for his drinking problem.

item.title: Companies hiring humans to train AI platforms with specific skills
item.description: AI training is an emerging job market where those with specialized skills are paid to train bots to be more accurate and human-like in their responses. The CBS News MoneyWatch team spoke with multiple hiring platforms that pair human experts with AI training jobs. Megan Cerullo has more.

item.title: Why Alex Murdaugh's murder conviction was overturned
item.description: Three years after Alex Murdaugh was convicted of killing his wife and son, the South Carolina Supreme Court on Wednesday overturned the verdict and granted Murdaugh a new trial. CBS News Katrina Kaufman reports.

item.title: California child sex abuse law brings justice for survivors, growing financial strain for schools
item.description: Survivors say decades-old school sex abuse lawsuits deliver overdue accountability. Schools say today's students are paying the price. CBS News California investigates.

item.title: DHS Secretary Mullin says ICE won't "round up" people at World Cup
item.description: Secretary of Homeland Security Markwayne Mullin told CBS News that ICE arrests at the FIFA World Cup are not off the table, but the agency will not be at the global sporting event for the purpose of immigration arrests.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: New York City blackout of 1977
item.description: On July 13, 1977, the great New York City blackout plunged the Big Apple into darkness and chaos throughout the very hot, humid night. Major Garrett introduces CBS News Radio's coverage of the outage that saw nearly 1,400 stores looted, more than 1,000 fires set and more than 3,700 people arrested.

item.title: Van Hollen posts his alcohol test results after Patel exchange
item.description: On Tuesday, Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen of Maryland challenged FBI Director Kash Patel to take a test to determine whether he has a drinking problem. Patel said he would if Van Hollen would, and on Wednesday, the senator posted his results. Nicholas Wu, congressional reporter for Semafor, and Daniella Diaz, politics reporter for NOTUS, join to discuss.

item.title: Markwayne Mullin details ICE's role at World Cup games
item.description: It's been less than two months since Markwayne Mullin was sworn in as the Secretary of Homeland Security. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga sat down with him for an exclusive interview.

item.title: Lutnick testified he knew little about Epstein, his next-door neighbor
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick said he was unaware for years that Jeffrey Epstein was a registered sex offender, according to a transcript of testimony released Wednesday.

item.title: How improved U.S.-China relationship could benefit the world
item.description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday morning ahead of a high-stakes meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. CBS News' Anna Coren and Natalie Brand report.

item.title: New book envisions future of war in world devastated by climate change
item.description: A new book by two decorated military officers offers a cautionary tale of warfare nearly 60 years in the future, in a world devastated by climate change. Elliot Ackerman and Ret. Navy Adm. James Stavridis join to discuss "2084: A Novel of Future War."

item.title: House leaders to take action on process for sexual misconduct claims
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson and Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries are launching a bipartisan task force aimed at addressing how sexual misconduct claims are handled within the House of Representatives, multiple sources confirm to CBS News.

item.title: Iran insists it's ready for a new U.S. attack as Trump arrives in China
item.description: Iran's military says it's trained and ready for any new U.S. assault as Trump predicts a "long talk" about the war with China's President Xi in Beijing.

item.title: Trump's plan to hold 30,000 migrants at Guantanamo yet to materialize after 1 year, documents show
item.description: President Trump promised to hold 30,000 migrants facing deportation at Guantanamo Bay. A year later, it's mostly empty. CBS News immigration and politics reporter Camilo Montoya-Galvez has the details.

item.title: How AI is changing travel
item.description: Almost every industry worldwide is integrating artificial intelligence into its operations, and the airline industry is no different. Booking.com CEO Glenn Fogel joins "The Takeout" to discuss AI's impact on travel.

item.title: What is "blue dot fever" and what does it mean for the music industry?
item.description: Major musicians from Post Malone to Meghan Trainor have recently struggled to sell out stadiums and arenas for their tours. It's a troubling trend being called "blue dot fever" and has led to entertainers canceling some or all of their shows. Ash-har Quraishi reports.

item.title: Trump's China trip underway as Americans grapple with higher costs
item.description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for a summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Mr. Trump's trip comes as Americans face higher costs for gas, food and more. CBS News' Anna Coren and Samantha Vinograd report.

item.title: Louisiana lawmakers advance new 5-1 congressional map that favors Republicans
item.description: The new map will eliminate one of the state's majority Black, Democratic-leaning districts while keeping one Democratic-leaning district.

item.title: Kouri Richins sentenced in husband's fentanyl-laced cocktail murder
item.description: Utah mom Kouri Richins was sentenced to life without the possibility of parole on Wednesday, after a jury convicted her of murder and other charges in her husband's 2022 death.

item.title: Elon Musk, Tim Cook, Jensen Huang accompany Trump in China
item.description: President Trump arrived in Beijing on Wednesday for his summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Accompanying the president are several high-profile business and tech CEOs. Chris McGuire, senior fellow at the Council on Foreign Relations, joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin doesn't rule out ICE arrests at World Cup
item.description: In an interview with CBS News' Nicole Sganga, Department of Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin said ICE arrests at the World Cup are not off the table. Sganga joins "The Takeout" with more on the conversation.

item.title: Kouri Richins reads statement addressed to her sons at sentencing hearing
item.description: Kouri Richins, the Utah mom convicted of murdering her husband, spoke for nearly 40 minutes at her sentencing hearing on Wednesday. Richins apologized to her sons and told them to "take care of one another." She was later sentenced to life without parole.

item.title: Kemp calls special legislative session to redraw Georgia's congressional map
item.description: Georgia lawmakers will return to the Capitol on June 17 for a special session focused on redistricting.

item.title: Tips to boost your phone's battery life
item.description: As smartphones become more advanced, are batteries keeping up? CNET senior mobile expert Abrar Al-Heeti joins to explain how you can improve your phone's battery life.

item.title: AI is fabricating citations in biomedical studies, researchers find
item.description: "Your doctor could be making decisions around treatment based on studies that never existed," one expert said.

item.title: Survivors of plane crash off Florida coast were on raft for hours, military says
item.description: The nail-biting incident took place about 80 miles east off the coast of Melbourne, Florida, which is about 175 miles north of Miami.

item.title: CEOs worth nearly $1 trillion accompany Trump to China
item.description: The delegation of business leaders underscores the deep ties many major U.S. companies maintain with China despite years of trade tensions.

item.title: Court overturns Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions, orders new trial
item.description: Alex Murdaugh was convicted of his killing his wife and his son at the family's home in 2021.

item.title: Princess Kate makes first overseas trip after cancer treatment
item.description: Catherine, the Princess of Wales, is in Italy for her first foreign trip after undergoing cancer treatment.

item.title: Few migrants held at Guantanamo, despite Trump's pledge
item.description: A CBS News review of internal government documents and information provided to Congress shows immigration detention facilities at Guantanamo Bay are nearly empty.
